title: Daily Briefing — Jun 29
headline: June swoon is landing right on cue: defensive flows and extreme fear rule the board.
body: Right, so the big picture: We're deep inside the midterm-year June swoon window (R521/R529), and the data lines up. Fear & Greed at 24.8 is screaming extreme fear (R013), VIX trending above its 20-day (R498), and SPY/QQQ are hugging their 20 MAs from below as tech sells off. Seasonality stats say 63% of midterm Junes finish red — don't treat this weakness as a contrarian buy, treat it as confirmation.

Key rotation: Tech (XLK, XLK $181.48) is weakening with broad leadership cracking — AAPL, NVDA, AMD, DELL, ARM all trading below their daily 20 MAs. Financials (XLF) and Communications (XLC) are lagging and showing high risk, while defensive sectors (XLU, XLP, XLV) are improving, but even those are not runaway safe havens given elevated risk readings. RRG quadrant shifts confirm the move: Industrials and Discretionary are the only bright spots, but nothing is leading strongly.

Contrarian/Risk: Extreme fear gauges usually mean opportunity, but this is an R521/R529 window — weakness is expected and can persist. Don't chase bounces here. If bonds were breaking (HYG, LQD), I'd reduce exposure, but they're stable for now. Any sustained VIX expansion will nod to further downside. Tech exhaustion, defensive drift, and midterm patterns all reinforce the risk — patience, react, don't predict.
callout_body:
SPY: 736.74 | 20 MA: 741.37 (watch for rejection)
QQQ: 713.09 | 20 MA: 722.94 (mean reversion zone)
AAPL: 280.43 | 20 MA: 296.71 (failed bounce)
HYG: 79.89 | 200 MA: 78.68 (risk trigger if breaks)
XLK: 181.48 | 20 MA: 186.61 (watch for further weakness)
Fear & Greed: 24.8 (extreme fear – sit tight)
VIX: 19.23 (> SMA20: 18.11, expansion confirming R498 seasonal volatility rise)
action_items: Do NOT chase any tech bounces – stay patient until daily closes recover 20 MA
Monitor bond ETFs (HYG, LQD) for breakdown; any slip below 200 MA = instant risk-off
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price — but this time, seasonal stats are in the ice bath with us.
